---
title: "Ladybug PopGen -- Handoff File"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float:
      collapsed: true
      smooth_scroll: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: default
---

# Ladybug PopGen -- Handoff File

**Last Updated:** 2026-04-14 (Tasks 4.1, 4.2, 4.3 all COMPLETE. Phase 4 GO enrichment pipeline finished end-to-end. Task 4.3 executed: 4 significant terms total across pattern x ontology at q<=0.05; results, simplified TSVs, and 17 plots written to results/enrichment/.)

**Purpose:** Operational coordination across Claude sessions. Tracks current development state, active tasks, blockers, and data contracts. Updated every session.

**Key principle:** The Master Doc (ladybug_master_doc.Rmd) is the architectural reference for the analysis pipeline, methodology, and results. This handoff is purely operational -- it tracks *what is happening now*, not *why things are designed the way they are*. Reference the Master Doc for explanations rather than restating them.

**Companion files (read on demand, not every session):**

- `ladybug_session_log.Rmd` -- Append-only session history. Read when catching up on recent implementation work.
- `ladybug_environment.Rmd` -- Platform, deployment, dependencies, VCS reference. Read when working with infrastructure or committing.
- `ladybug_file_map.Rmd` -- Directory tree and scripts/files reference. Read when creating or locating files.

---

### DOCUMENT MAINTENANCE RULES:
- **ADD** new information to appropriate sections; do not remove existing content unless archiving
- **EDIT** only when specific information needs correction
- **REFERENCE** the Master Doc for design rationale (format: [See Master Doc Section X.X: Title])

---

# 1. Claude Code Session Protocol

**AT SESSION START:**

- Read this Handoff File (all sections)
- Check the Session Log (`ladybug_session_log.Rmd`) to understand recent work
- Read the Master Doc if instructed or if the task requires design context

**DURING THE SESSION:**

- Follow the QC check protocol below -- pause between major steps, report metrics, and ask before proceeding
- When referencing methodology or design rationale, check the Master Doc
- When implementing cross-step interfaces, check "Integration Points" (Section 5) for data contracts
- If a step's expected inputs or outputs change during implementation, update Section 5 immediately
- Log work as you go in the "Current Session" section (Section 6) below

**AT SESSION END:**

Flush the current session to the session log, then clean up:

1. **Write to session log first:** Read only the first ~20 lines of `ladybug_session_log.Rmd` to confirm the structure. Insert the contents of Section 6 (everything between `[SESSION START]` and `[SESSION END]`) immediately after the `# Active Session Log` header, above any existing entries. Do not read the rest of the file.
2. **Clear Section 6:** Replace the session content with the empty template (keep the `[SESSION START]` and `[SESSION END]` markers, remove everything between them).
3. **Update other sections as needed:**
   - Update "Current Development State" (Section 2) if statuses changed
   - Move completed tasks to "Completed Tasks" (Section 3.3) with date
   - Update the File Map (`ladybug_file_map.Rmd`) if files were created, moved, or deleted
   - Flag any issues or questions in Section 4
   - If a significant design decision was made, note it and flag it for promotion to the Project History Decision Register

**Important:** Always write to the session log before clearing Section 6. If the session is interrupted, a duplicate entry is recoverable; a lost entry is not.

### QC Check Protocol (Mandatory)

- Pause between major implementation steps to verify outputs before proceeding
- Report QC metrics after each step -- do not continue silently
- Ask the user if results look reasonable before moving to the next part
- If any test fails or produces unexpected results, STOP and report

**Check-in format after major steps:**
```
Step [X] complete.
  Results:
  - Output file: [path]
  - [Key metric 1]: [value] (expected: [range])
  - [Key metric 2]: [value] (expected: [range])
  QC Status: [PASS / WARNING / FAIL]
  [Notes or concerns]
  Ready to proceed to Step [Y]. Continue? (y/n)
```

**Red flags to watch for and report:**

- Dimension mismatches (wrong number of SNPs, samples, chromosomes)
- Empty output files or zero counts
- Extreme values outside expected ranges (see Section 5.2)
- Schema mismatches between pipeline steps (check Section 5)
- Missing data artifacts (especially in CHI population, effective n ~ 3.3)
- Outputs that differ dramatically from previous similar analyses

---

# 2. Current Development State

## 2.1 Phase Overview

| Phase | Description | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| Phase 1 | Population diversity analysis (Tajima's D, pi) | Complete | Nov-Dec 2025 |
| Phase 2 | Population differentiation (FST) | Complete | Dual approach: genome-wide + windowed |
| Phase 3 | Selection scan (outlier detection) | Complete | Per-SNP percentile, OutFLANK, pcadapt, cross-method concordance all done |
| Phase 4 | Gene annotation and functional analysis | In progress | Enrichment decisions resolved; Tasks 4.1-4.3 spec'd (2026-04-13) |
| Phase 5 | Manuscript preparation | Not started | Waiting on Phase 3-4 completion |

**Current phase:** Phase 4 in progress.

**Current focus:** GO enrichment pipeline implementation. Task 4.1 (SNP-to-gene mapping) complete 2026-04-14: 2,648 foreground genes, 11,105 background genes, 93.7% of high-confidence SNPs map to a gene. Task 4.2 (gene-to-GO mapping) implementation complete (`scripts/gene_to_go_mapping.py`) with persistent cache + MD5-based invalidation; Route A (NCBI direct) validated locally at 7,644/11,105 = 68.8% background coverage, already above the >=60% target. Route B (two-step BLAST orthology transfer) is implemented but blocked locally by a `makeblastdb` path-with-spaces bug; planned to run on the cluster (see Blockers section). Next: fix Route B, then Task 4.3 (ORA enrichment stratified by pattern). Foreground: n_methods >= 2 (3,205 SNPs). Background: testable gene universe. GO only (KEGG deferred).

## 2.2 Analysis Component Status

| Component | Phase | Status | Last Touched | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| Diversity (Tajima's D, pi) | 1 | Complete | 2025-12-08 | May need re-run if samples excluded |
| FST (genome-wide + windowed) | 2 | Complete | 2026-01-13 | fst.sh refactored; summarize_fst.R created |
| Unified percentile scan (windowed) | 3 | SUPERSEDED | 2026-01-09 | Replaced by per-SNP percentile approach (2026-04-13) |
| Per-SNP percentile scan | 3 | Complete | 2026-04-13 | 21,534 autosomal outliers (1%, CHI-filtered) |
| CHI genotype count filter | 3 | Complete | 2026-04-13 | Applied to CHI-vs-invasive comparisons (>=3 of 4 CHI genotyped) |
| OutFLANK diagnostics | 3 | Complete | 2026-01-12 | Validation role only; now directly comparable at SNP level |
| pcadapt scan | 3 | Complete | 2026-04-13 | K=2, 35,881 autosomal outliers (q<0.05) |
| Sample QC | 3 | Complete | 2026-01-13 | 7 samples excluded (>50% missing); 81 retained |
| Cross-method concordance | 3 | Complete | 2026-04-13 | 54,965 candidates: Tier 1=2,474, Tier 2=17,759, Tier 3=34,732 |
| GEA (RDA/LFMM) | 3 | Planning | 2026-04-13 | Feasibility assessed; viable for among-invasive comparisons |
| Gene annotation (BLAST) | 4 | Needs revamp | 2025-12-08 | Two-step complete on old outliers; deferred pending enrichment pipeline |
| SNP-to-gene mapping | 4 | Complete | 2026-04-14 | 2,648 foreground / 11,105 background genes; R implementation (no BEDTools dep); 93.7% SNP mapping rate |
| Gene-to-GO mapping | 4 | Spec'd | 2026-04-13 | Route A (NCBI gene2go direct) + Route B (orthology transfer via BLAST to T. castaneum) |
| GO enrichment (ORA) | 4 | Spec'd | 2026-04-13 | clusterProfiler enricher(); stratified by pattern; testable-gene background |
| Functional categorization | 4 | Needs revamp | 2025-12-08 | Dependent on updated annotation of new outlier set |

---

# 3. Task Queue

## 3.1 Pending Tasks

### Task 4.1: SNP-to-Gene Mapping [COMPLETE 2026-04-14]
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** High
- **Phase:** 4
- **Component:** SNP-to-gene mapping
- **Status:** COMPLETE. Implemented as `scripts/snp_to_gene_mapping.R` (R/data.table non-equi join rather than BEDTools, since BEDTools was not installed and an R implementation fit the existing pipeline better). Outputs written to `results/enrichment/`. Results: 2,648 foreground genes, 11,105 background genes, 93.7% of 3,205 high-confidence SNPs mapped to a gene. Validation suite (30 structural/content/spot-check tests) all passed; the single "FAIL" (no foreground genes on NC_058198.1) reflects biology, not a bug -- that chromosome has zero n_methods>=2 outliers.
- **Description:** Map outlier SNPs to genes using BEDTools intersect against the C. septempunctata GFF annotation. Use asymmetric flanking: 5 kb upstream, 2 kb downstream of each gene (captures insect promoter regions without excessive noise). Build TWO gene lists: (1) foreground (genes containing or near high-confidence outlier SNPs, n_methods >= 2), and (2) background/universe (all genes containing or near ANY autosomal SNP in the filtered dataset). The background defines the testable gene universe for enrichment, which is the current field standard for ORA in selection scan studies.
- **Context/References:** The high-confidence outlier set is 3,205 SNPs (2+ methods from cross-method concordance). Full outlier set is 54,965 SNPs (available as fallback if annotation attrition leaves too few foreground genes). Gene body coordinates come from the GFF `gene` features. Flanking is asymmetric because insect promoters are typically 1-5 kb upstream; downstream regulatory elements are less common. The background must be the testable gene universe (genes with at least one SNP), NOT all genes in the genome, to avoid inflating the denominator with untestable genes and creating spurious enrichments.
- **Inputs:**
  - `results/cross_method_concordance/master_candidate_table.tsv` (54,965 rows; filter n_methods >= 2 for foreground)
  - `data/plink/ladybug_snps.bim` (full SNP universe, 803,821 SNPs; exclude NC_058189.1 for autosomes only)
  - `data/coccinella_septempunctata_/ncbi_dataset/data/GCF_907165205.1/genomic.gff` (gene annotations)
- **Outputs:** All outputs in `results/enrichment/`
  - `foreground_genes.tsv`: gene_id, chrom, gene_start, gene_end, strand, gene_name (if available), n_outlier_snps, consensus_pattern (from master table), detecting_methods, location_type (genic/upstream/downstream)
  - `background_genes.tsv`: gene_id, chrom, gene_start, gene_end, strand, gene_name, n_total_snps (all SNPs, not just outliers)
  - `snp_to_gene_mapping.tsv`: snp_id, chrom, pos, gene_id, location_type, distance_to_gene (0 if genic)
  - `snp_gene_summary.txt`: counts of genic vs intergenic SNPs, number of unique foreground genes, number of background genes, annotation coverage metrics
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Report: N foreground genes, N background genes, % of high-confidence SNPs that map to a gene (genic + flanking), % that are intergenic
  - Each gene counted once in foreground regardless of how many SNPs hit it, but n_outlier_snps preserved as metadata
  - Consensus pattern per gene: if a gene has SNPs from multiple patterns, assign the majority pattern; if tied, assign BOTH
  - Background must include foreground genes (foreground is a subset of background)
  - Red flag: foreground gene count < 100 (may need to expand to full outlier set)
  - Red flag: >80% of SNPs intergenic (would suggest flanking window is too small, or annotation is sparse)
- **Dependencies:** Cross-method concordance (complete). GFF annotation file (available). BEDTools must be installed or installable in conda env.

### Task 4.2: Gene-to-GO Term Mapping
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** High
- **Phase:** 4
- **Component:** Gene-to-GO mapping
- **Description:** Map foreground and background genes to GO terms using two routes. Route A: direct lookup of C. septempunctata Gene IDs in NCBI gene2go. Route B: orthology transfer via existing two-step BLAST results (C. septempunctata -> H. axyridis -> T. castaneum), inheriting GO terms from the best ortholog hit. Both routes contribute to a unified gene-to-GO table. Each gene-GO association should be annotated with its source (direct vs orthology) and the ortholog identity if transferred. **Caching:** Maintain a persistent gene-GO cache (`gene_go_cache.tsv`) that maps gene_id to its full set of GO annotations. On each run, load the cache, diff against the current gene list, annotate only genes not already cached, append new results, and write the cache back. This avoids re-annotating the ~5,000-15,000 background genes when re-running after upstream changes (e.g., modified flanking window, expanded foreground). The cache key is gene_id; the GO mapping for a given gene does not depend on whether it is foreground or background.
- **Context/References:** C. septempunctata is a non-model organism. Direct gene2go coverage is expected to be sparse (~56% from earlier analysis). Orthology transfer via T. castaneum fills the gap but introduces uncertainty, especially for Biological Process terms which are less sequence-conserved than Molecular Function terms. The existing BLAST results and GO mapping files are in `data/go_annotations/`. The two-step BLAST cascade produced 89% success for H. axyridis and 77% for T. castaneum. All three GO ontologies should be included: Biological Process (BP), Molecular Function (MF), Cellular Component (CC).
- **Inputs:**
  - `results/enrichment/foreground_genes.tsv` (from Task 4.1)
  - `results/enrichment/background_genes.tsv` (from Task 4.1)
  - `data/go_annotations/gene2go` (NCBI direct mapping)
  - `data/go_annotations/tribolium_gene2go.tsv` (T. castaneum GO terms)
  - `data/go_annotations/tribolium_protein2gene.tsv` (T. castaneum protein-to-gene map)
  - `data/go_annotations/harmonia_protein2gene.tsv` (H. axyridis protein-to-gene map)
  - Existing BLAST output files (location TBD by Claude Code; likely in `data/blast_db/` or `results/` from the December 2025 annotation run)
- **Outputs:** All outputs in `results/enrichment/`
  - `gene2go_foreground.tsv`: gene_id, go_id, go_term, go_ontology (BP/MF/CC), evidence_source (direct/orthology_Haxy/orthology_Tcas), ortholog_id, blast_identity (if transferred)
  - `gene2go_background.tsv`: same schema, for all background genes
  - `gene_go_cache.tsv`: persistent cache of all gene-GO mappings computed across runs. Same schema as gene2go tables plus a `cache_date` column. This file grows across runs and is never regenerated from scratch unless invalidated.
  - `gene_go_cache_meta.txt`: records the input file hashes (MD5 of gene2go, tribolium_gene2go.tsv, and BLAST output files) used to populate the cache. If any input file changes, the cache is stale and should be regenerated.
  - `go_mapping_summary.txt`: annotation coverage (% of foreground genes with >= 1 GO term, broken down by source route), number of unique GO terms, coverage by ontology, cache hit/miss statistics
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Report annotation coverage: % foreground genes with at least one GO term (target: >60%; red flag: <40%)
  - Report coverage by route: how many genes annotated by Route A only, Route B only, both
  - If annotation coverage < 40%, flag for InterProScan consideration as a future step
  - GO terms from both routes should be merged (union) per gene; if a gene gets the same GO term from both routes, keep both evidence sources but count the term once
  - **Cache behavior:** On first run (no cache exists), annotate all genes and write the cache. On subsequent runs, report how many genes were served from cache vs newly annotated. If input file hashes in `gene_go_cache_meta.txt` don't match current input files, log a warning and regenerate the cache from scratch. The script should accept a `--force-rebuild` flag (or equivalent) to bypass the cache when needed.
- **Dependencies:** Task 4.1 (foreground and background gene lists). Existing BLAST results and GO data files. Claude Code should verify that BLAST output files from the December 2025 run are still present and in the expected format; if not, flag as a blocker.

### Task 4.3: GO Enrichment Analysis (ORA, Stratified by Pattern)
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** High
- **Phase:** 4
- **Component:** GO enrichment (ORA)
- **Description:** Run over-representation analysis (ORA) using clusterProfiler's `enricher()` function with custom gene-to-GO annotation. Use the testable gene universe (background_genes with GO terms) as the background. Run enrichment separately for each pattern category (INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH) and for the combined foreground set. Apply Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction. Collapse redundant GO terms using `rrvgo` (REVIGO-like local R implementation; uses org.Dm.eg.db as phylogenetic proxy for information content since no OrgDb exists for C. septempunctata or T. castaneum on Bioconductor). Generate publication-quality plots including rrvgo's treemap and scatterplot for semantic visualization.
- **Context/References:** ORA tests whether GO terms are overrepresented among outlier genes vs the testable background using Fisher's exact test (hypergeometric). This is the standard approach for selection scan studies (as opposed to GSEA, which requires a continuous ranking). Stratification by pattern category is the key analytical contribution: it asks whether different biological processes are associated with different phases of invasion (initial colonization vs post-invasion local adaptation). The BOTH category may be too small for meaningful enrichment depending on gene counts; report the null result if so. Each ontology (BP, MF, CC) should be tested separately. BP is the most biologically interpretable for invasion biology questions; MF and CC provide supporting evidence.
- **Inputs:**
  - `results/enrichment/foreground_genes.tsv` (from Task 4.1; contains consensus_pattern column)
  - `results/enrichment/gene2go_foreground.tsv` (from Task 4.2)
  - `results/enrichment/gene2go_background.tsv` (from Task 4.2)
  - `results/enrichment/background_genes.tsv` (from Task 4.1)
- **Outputs:** All outputs in `results/enrichment/`
  - `enrichment_results_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv` for each pattern (ALL, INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH) x ontology (BP, MF, CC): GO ID, GO term, gene ratio, bg ratio, p-value, q-value (BH-adjusted), gene list
  - `enrichment_results_simplified_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`: after redundancy reduction via rrvgo (columns: parent_go_id, parent_term, cluster, score)
  - `rrvgo_sim_matrix_{pattern}_{ontology}.rds`: cached semantic similarity matrix for reproducibility
  - `enrichment_summary.txt`: number of significant terms (q < 0.05) per pattern x ontology combination; number of foreground genes per pattern; annotation coverage per pattern
  - Plots in `results/enrichment/plots/`:
    - `dotplot_{pattern}_{ontology}.png`: top 15-20 enriched terms, dot size = gene ratio, color = q-value
    - `barplot_top_terms_{pattern}.png`: combined across ontologies, top 10 terms per pattern
    - `comparison_dotplot.png`: compareCluster-style plot showing enrichment across pattern categories side by side (BP only, for manuscript figure candidate)
    - `rrvgo_treemap_{pattern}_{ontology}.png`: rrvgo treemap showing parent-term clustering (BP primarily)
    - `rrvgo_scatter_{pattern}_{ontology}.png`: rrvgo MDS scatterplot of semantic space
  - `enrichment_log.txt`: full analysis log with parameters, warnings, and diagnostics
- **Acceptance criteria:**
  - Report number of significant terms at q < 0.05 for each pattern x ontology combination
  - Report foreground gene count per pattern (if any pattern has < 20 annotated genes, flag as underpowered and note in log)
  - Background should be all background genes that have at least one GO term (not all background genes, since genes without GO terms are untestable by ORA)
  - If zero significant terms for a pattern at q < 0.05, report q < 0.10 as suggestive and note in summary
  - Check for suspiciously broad terms (e.g., "metabolic process," "binding") dominating the top results, which may indicate background bias; report if found
  - Generate comparison plot across patterns even if some patterns have no significant terms (shows the contrast)
- **Dependencies:** Tasks 4.1 and 4.2 complete. R packages: clusterProfiler, enrichplot, rrvgo, org.Dm.eg.db, GOSemSim. Install via BiocManager if missing. Note: rrvgo requires an OrgDb for information content calculation; using org.Dm.eg.db (D. melanogaster) as phylogenetic proxy since no Coleoptera OrgDb is available on Bioconductor. This proxy is a limitation to note in the manuscript.

### GEA Feasibility and Planning
- **Assigned to:** User (Rei) / Claude.ai -- planning; Claude Code -- implementation
- **Priority:** Medium
- **Phase:** 3
- **Component:** GEA (RDA/LFMM)
- **Description:** Assess feasibility and implement genotype-environment association analysis. Sample coordinate data available (C7_samples_locations_updated.xlsx). 21 unique sampling sites across 4 continents. Extract WorldClim bioclimatic variables for each site. Run RDA (primary) and optionally LFMM (complement) to identify SNPs associated with environmental gradients.
- **Context/References:** Geographic assessment (2026-04-13): CHI = 1 site (Beijing), no within-population environmental variation. EEU = 4 sites, Crete to Estonia (23 degrees latitude). WEU = 5 sites, Spain to Denmark (13 degrees latitude). USA = 10 sites, KY/VA to NY plus Sacramento CA (major east-west gradient). GEA is viable for among-invasive comparisons (POST_INVASION category) but NOT for CHI-vs-invasive (single CHI site = no environmental variation in ancestral range). CHI samples should be excluded from GEA analysis or included only as a conditioning variable.
- **Acceptance criteria:** Environmental variables extracted and screened for collinearity. RDA run with appropriate population structure correction (partial RDA conditioning on neutral PCs). Candidate SNPs identified and cross-referenced with other method results. Report which environmental variables drive the strongest associations.
- **Dependencies:** Sample exclusion decision. LD-pruned dataset (RDA assumes independence among loci). Coordinate data (available). WorldClim data extraction.

### Three-Tier BLAST Annotation (Deferred)
- **Assigned to:** Claude Code
- **Priority:** Low (deferred)
- **Phase:** 4
- **Component:** Gene annotation
- **Description:** Upgrade annotation from two-step to three-tier BLAST cascade by adding D. melanogaster (>=30% identity, E-value <=1e-5). Deferred until after enrichment pipeline completes; enrichment proceeds with existing two-step BLAST results.
- **Context/References:** See Master Doc for phylogenetic rationale and identity thresholds.
- **Dependencies:** Enrichment pipeline completion (Tasks 4.1-4.3). Only pursue if GO annotation coverage is insufficient (<40%).

## 3.2 Decisions and Designs for Review

### Shift from Windowed to Per-SNP Percentile (Decision Made 2026-04-13)

**Decision:** Replace windowed FST percentile approach (5kb windows) with per-SNP FST percentile as the primary FST-based detection method. Windowed FST retained only for visualization (Manhattan plots).

**Rationale:** The windowed approach created a scale mismatch with OutFLANK and pcadapt (both SNP-level), resulting in 3-4% concordance that reflected aggregation artifacts rather than real methodological disagreement. Per-SNP detection puts all three methods on the same scale, enabling meaningful concordance calculation. Trade-off: per-SNP FST is noisier than windowed FST, especially for CHI-vs-invasive comparisons (CHI n=4). Mitigated by the CHI genotype count filter (require >=3 of 4 CHI individuals genotyped) and post-hoc clustering of nearby outlier SNPs into regions.

**Implications:** unified_selection_scan.R results (1,963 outlier windows at 1%) are superseded. Pattern categorization framework (BOTH/INVASION_SPECIFIC/POST_INVASION) is retained but applied at the SNP level. Previous OutFLANK concordance numbers (3-4%) are no longer relevant; new SNP-level concordance expected to be higher if methods detect similar signals.

**Flag for Project History Decision Register.**

### GEA Feasibility Assessment (2026-04-13)

**Finding:** Sample coordinate data reveals 21 unique sampling sites. CHI = 1 site (Beijing), which rules out GEA for the CHI-vs-invasive axis (no within-native environmental variation). However, invasive populations span substantial environmental gradients: EEU from Crete (35N) to Estonia (58N), WEU from Spain (43N) to Denmark (56N), USA from KY/VA to NY plus Sacramento CA (major continental gradient). GEA via RDA (and possibly LFMM) is viable for detecting environment-associated variation within the invasive range, complementing the POST_INVASION outlier category.

**Recommendation:** Proceed with GEA as an additional analysis layer. Use partial RDA conditioning on neutral PCs to separate environmental associations from demographic structure. Focus interpretation on among-invasive environmental gradients. Cross-reference GEA candidates with per-SNP percentile, OutFLANK, and pcadapt results.

**Flag for Project History Decision Register.**

### GO Enrichment Pipeline Design (DECIDED 2026-04-13)

**Decision:** Implement a three-step GO enrichment pipeline: (1) SNP-to-gene mapping via BEDTools intersect with asymmetric flanking (5 kb upstream, 2 kb downstream), (2) gene-to-GO mapping via NCBI gene2go (direct) plus orthology transfer through existing two-step BLAST cascade, (3) over-representation analysis (ORA) via clusterProfiler enricher() with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR, stratified by pattern category.

**Foreground definition:** High-confidence outlier SNPs (n_methods >= 2 from cross-method concordance, 3,205 SNPs). This is the field-standard "detected by 2+ methods" criterion (e.g., de Jong et al. 2021, Dalongeville et al. 2018). Fallback to full outlier set (54,965) if annotation attrition leaves < 100 foreground genes.

**Background definition:** All genes containing at least one SNP in the filtered dataset (testable gene universe). This controls for ascertainment bias and gene-length effects. Using all genome genes would inflate the denominator with untestable genes and create spurious enrichments.

**Flanking window rationale:** 5 kb upstream / 2 kb downstream is asymmetric to reflect insect promoter architecture. Insect core promoter elements are typically within 1-2 kb, but distal enhancers can extend to 5 kb. Downstream regulatory elements are less common.

**Stratification rationale:** Running enrichment separately for INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, and BOTH patterns tests whether different biological processes associate with different phases of invasion. Combined (ALL) enrichment provides the overall picture. Pattern categories with < 20 annotated foreground genes are flagged as underpowered.

**GO mapping approach:** Route A (NCBI gene2go direct) provides highest confidence but sparse coverage (~56%). Route B (orthology transfer via BLAST to T. castaneum) fills the gap via the existing two-step cascade. InterProScan and three-tier BLAST (adding D. melanogaster) are deferred as future improvements.

**Redundancy reduction:** rrvgo (2026-04-14 revision). Initially spec'd clusterProfiler::simplify() but that requires an OrgDb for GOSemSim, which doesn't exist for C. septempunctata or T. castaneum on Bioconductor. rrvgo is a REVIGO-like Bioconductor package that performs scripted, reproducible GO term clustering locally. Uses org.Dm.eg.db (D. melanogaster) as phylogenetic proxy for information content calculation. Produces treemap and MDS scatterplot visualizations beyond what simplify() offers. Limitations: D. melanogaster IC values are an imperfect proxy for beetle-specific information content; acceptable compromise given the alternative (building a custom OrgDb via AnnotationForge is non-trivial).

**Alternatives considered:**
  - Tier 1+2 as foreground (20,233 SNPs) instead of 2+ methods (3,205 SNPs): rejected because the tier system has no field precedent and Tier 2 includes single-method SNPs with "strong support," which is less defensible than requiring independent method confirmation
  - KEGG pathway enrichment: deferred; adds manuscript scope without clear necessity for a course paper
  - GSEA instead of ORA: rejected; requires continuous ranking of all genes, which is non-standard for discrete outlier lists from selection scans
  - Gene length bias correction via goseq: deferred; testable-gene-universe background is the primary control, and goseq adds complexity
  - InterProScan for domain-based GO assignment: deferred; computationally heavier, orthology transfer sufficient for current scope
  - Whole-genome background: rejected; inflates denominator with untestable genes

**Limitations to acknowledge in manuscript:**
  - Orthology-transferred GO terms (especially Biological Process) are hypotheses, not confirmed annotations. Confidence degrades with phylogenetic distance.
  - The testable gene universe depends on SNP density, which varies across the genome. Gene-poor regions are underrepresented.
  - Small foreground sizes per pattern category may limit power for pattern-stratified enrichment.

**Flag for Project History Decision Register.**

### Sample Exclusion Strategy (DECIDED 2026-04-13)

**Decision:** Exclude 7 samples with >50% missingness. 4 borderline samples (20-50%) kept after PCA inspection showed no drift (Yi 2022).

## 3.3 Completed Tasks

- **2026-04-14:** Task 4.1 (SNP-to-gene mapping) complete. Implemented in R (data.table non-equi join) rather than BEDTools. Outputs: 2,648 foreground genes, 11,105 background genes, 3,002/3,205 high-confidence SNPs (93.7%) mapped. Pattern distribution of foreground genes: POST_INVASION 1,410, INVASION_SPECIFIC 696, DISCORDANT 365, BOTH 177. Location breakdown: 3,062 genic, 1,628 upstream, 620 downstream. Foreground gene count (2,648) exceeds handoff's 200-800 estimate but is consistent with input SNP count and gene-dense beetle genome. Validated with 30-test suite (all structural, cross-reference, strand-aware flanking, consensus-pattern-majority-rule, and background-SNP-count checks passed).
- **2026-04-13:** Phase 3 complete. Per-SNP FST percentile scan (21,534 autosomal outliers at 1%, CHI-filtered). pcadapt scan (K=2, 35,881 outliers at q<0.05). Cross-method concordance: 54,965 total candidates tiered (Tier 1: 2,474; Tier 2: 17,759; Tier 3: 34,732). Three-way overlap: 64 SNPs. Pattern distribution: INVASION_SPECIFIC 64%, POST_INVASION 22%, BOTH 13%. Results in results/cross_method_concordance/. Phase 4 enrichment planning started.
- **2026-01-13:** PLINK conversion completed (803,821 variants x 88 individuals, 87.4% genotyping rate). Sample QC validation completed (11 samples >20% missing identified). fst.sh refactored (851 -> 165 lines) and summarize_fst.R created.
- **2026-01-12:** OutFLANK SNP ID format fixed (CHROM:POS). Concordance validation completed (EEU vs WEU: 3.11%, EEU vs USA: 3.87%).
- **2026-01-09:** Genome-wide FST calculated (CHI vs invasive: 0.14-0.15). OutFLANK diagnostic on all 6 comparisons. Unified percentile scan completed (1,963 outlier windows at 1%).
- **2025-12-08:** Diversity analysis finalized. FST analysis completed. Gene annotation pipeline (two-step BLAST). Functional categorization.
- **2025-12-06:** Selection scan dual-method approach (superseded by unified percentile 2026-01-09). Pattern categorization system. Cross-threshold validation.
- **2025-11-17 to 2025-11-20:** Project initiation. Diversity pipeline development. Script standardization.

---

# 4. Open Questions and Blockers

## 4.1 Blockers

- **[2026-04-14] Task 4.2 Route B (BLAST orthology transfer) blocked locally.** `makeblastdb` fails when the output path contains spaces (the iCloud project root has them); error message: `No alias or index file found for protein database [/Users/reina/Library/Mobile]`. Subprocess passes quoted strings, so this is `makeblastdb` mishandling its own internal path parsing. Fix options: (a) write the BLAST DB to `/tmp/blast_db_biol624/` (matches prior Dec 2025 script pattern), then use the /tmp path for queries; (b) move the run to the cluster (preferred, also faster). Route A alone already exceeds the >=60% coverage target (68.8%), so Task 4.3 is not strictly blocked, but Route B should still be added before final analysis.

## 4.2 Open Questions

- **[2026-01-13] Should earlier analyses (diversity, FST) be re-run after sample exclusion?** VCF-level filtering may have already mitigated most impact, but cleaner estimates would strengthen the manuscript.
- **[2026-01-13] Impact of CHN11 on existing results.** CHN11 (60% missing) was included in all prior analyses. With effective CHI n ~ 3.3, how reliable are CHI-specific Tajima's D and pi estimates?
- **[2026-04-13] Optimal clustering distance for post-hoc SNP region merging.** Per-SNP outliers will be clustered into regions for biological interpretation. Need to determine appropriate distance threshold (5kb? 10kb? LD-based?). Should be informed by LD decay estimates if available.
- **[2026-04-13] LD pruning strategy for pcadapt and RDA.** Both methods assume approximate independence among loci. Options: (a) LD-prune before running (cleaner, but reduces SNP count), (b) run on full dataset and clump afterward (preserves data, but inflates outlier counts). Note: LD-pruned dataset exists (~37M SNPs in C7_filtered_ldpruned.vcf.gz) but appears to be from unfiltered data and may need re-pruning from the filtered set.
- **[2026-04-13] Which environmental variables for GEA?** Need to extract WorldClim bioclimatic variables and screen for collinearity. Key candidates: annual mean temperature, temperature seasonality, annual precipitation, precipitation seasonality. Must assess collinearity with population structure.

## 4.3 Resolved Questions

- **[2026-04-13] Enrichment foreground scope:** Resolved. Use n_methods >= 2 (3,205 SNPs) as foreground, consistent with field-standard "detected by 2+ methods" criterion. Fallback to full 54,965 if gene count < 100.
- **[2026-04-14] DISCORDANT handling in Task 4.3:** Resolved. DISCORDANT genes (n=365 at gene level) are retained only in the ALL foreground and excluded from pattern-stratified runs (INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH). Rationale: DISCORDANT reflects methodological disagreement across detection methods, not a coherent biological pattern; reassigning to BOTH would contaminate BOTH with methodologically ambiguous genes. Encoded in `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` via the `foreground_sets` list.
- **[2026-04-13] Enrichment method:** Resolved. GO enrichment via ORA (clusterProfiler enricher()) as primary. KEGG deferred.
- **[2026-04-13] Background gene set:** Resolved. Testable gene universe (all genes near any SNP in the filtered dataset). Controls for ascertainment bias and gene-length effects.
- **[2026-04-13] Pattern stratification:** Resolved. Run enrichment separately for ALL, INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH. Report all results including null findings.
- **[2026-04-13] Windowed vs per-SNP percentile approach:** Resolved by shifting to per-SNP. Windowed approach created scale mismatch with SNP-level methods. Per-SNP with CHI genotype count filter and post-hoc clustering addresses noise concerns while enabling direct cross-method concordance.
- **[2026-04-13] GEA feasibility:** Viable for among-invasive comparisons (21 sites, substantial environmental gradients in EEU, WEU, USA). Not viable for CHI-vs-invasive (single site in Beijing, no within-native environmental variation).
- **[2026-01-09] OutFLANK failure on CHI vs invasive:** Not a failure -- excellent fit (df=2) but FSTbar (~0.11) approaches observed FST, so no SNPs exceed the inflated threshold. Resolved by adopting unified percentile as primary method.
- **[2026-01-09] Windowed FST values too low:** Windowed mean FST (0.05-0.06) is expected for window-averaged values. Genome-wide weighted FST (0.14-0.15) matches literature. Both valid for different purposes.
- **[2026-01-12] OutFLANK-percentile concordance low (3-4%):** Reflects fundamental methodological differences (SNP-level vs window-level), not analytical failure. Further resolved by shift to per-SNP approach (2026-04-13).

---

# 5. Integration Points

## 5.1 Data Contracts

### VCF Files --> PLINK Conversion
- **Producer:** Pre-project VCF filtering (chr_filter.sh)
- **Consumer:** PLINK for pcadapt input
- **Format:** Per-chromosome gzipped VCF (10 files)
- **Location:** `data/VARIANTS_BY_CHR/FILTERED_CHR_VCFS/*.filtered.vcf.gz`
- **Schema:** Standard VCF with 88 samples, biallelic SNPs only
- **Notes:** WG_VARIANTS is UNFILTERED -- never use directly. Must use per-chromosome filtered VCFs.

### PLINK Files --> pcadapt
- **Producer:** PLINK conversion (merged from per-chromosome VCFs)
- **Consumer:** pcadapt R package
- **Format:** PLINK v1.9 binary (bed/bim/fam)
- **Location:** `data/plink/ladybug_snps.bed/bim/fam`
- **Schema:** 803,821 variants x 88 individuals. Variant IDs as CHROM:POS (e.g., `NC_058190.1:12345`). 87.4% genotyping rate.
- **Notes:** If samples excluded, regenerate as `ladybug_snps_filtered.bed/bim/fam`. pcadapt requires PLINK v1.9 format (not PLINK 2).

### Windowed FST --> Percentile Selection Scan (SUPERSEDED)
- **Status:** SUPERSEDED by per-SNP approach (2026-04-13). Retained for visualization only.
- **Producer:** fst.sh (Phase 2, windowed mode)
- **Consumer:** Manhattan plot generation only (no longer used for detection)
- **Format:** TSV (`.windowed.weir.fst` files)
- **Location:** `results/fst_*_vs_*_results/fst/`
- **Schema:** CHROM, BIN_START, BIN_END, N_VARIANTS, WEIGHTED_FST, MEAN_FST (5kb windows)

### Per-SNP FST --> Per-SNP Percentile Selection Scan (NEW)
- **Producer:** vcftools `--weir-fst-pop` (no window flag)
- **Consumer:** per-SNP percentile scan script (to be created), cross-method concordance script
- **Format:** TSV (`.weir.fst` files)
- **Location:** `results/fst_*_vs_*_results/fst_per_snp/` (to be created)
- **Schema:** CHROM, POS, WEIR_AND_COCKERHAM_FST (one row per SNP per comparison)
- **Notes:** ~800K rows per comparison, 6 comparisons. Negative FST values are expected and valid. For CHI-vs-invasive comparisons, also need per-site sample count to apply the CHI genotype count filter.

### Outlier Windows --> Gene Annotation (SUPERSEDED)
- **Status:** SUPERSEDED. Gene annotation will consume per-SNP outlier lists instead.
- **Producer:** unified_selection_scan.R
- **Consumer:** annotate_outliers_local.py (or future three-tier BLAST script)
- **Format:** TSV
- **Location:** `results/unified_selection_scan/outliers_*pct.tsv`
- **Schema:** window_id, chrom, start, end, comparison, fst, threshold, pattern, confidence

### Per-SNP Outliers --> Gene Annotation (NEW)
- **Status:** SUPERSEDED by enrichment pipeline (Tasks 4.1-4.3). See contracts below.

### Cross-Method Concordance --> SNP-to-Gene Mapping (Task 4.1)
- **Producer:** cross_method_concordance.R
- **Consumer:** Task 4.1 SNP-to-gene mapping script
- **Format:** TSV
- **Location:** `results/cross_method_concordance/master_candidate_table.tsv`
- **Schema:** chrom, pos, snp_id, percentile_outlier, percentile_comparisons, percentile_fst_max, percentile_pattern, outflank_outlier, outflank_comparisons, outflank_q_min, pcadapt_outlier, pcadapt_q, pcadapt_z_PC1, pcadapt_z_PC2, pcadapt_pc_category, n_methods, method_families, methods_available, confidence_tier, consensus_pattern
- **Filtering for foreground:** n_methods >= 2 (yields 3,205 SNPs: 3,141 by exactly 2 methods + 64 by all 3)
- **Pattern column:** consensus_pattern (INVASION_SPECIFIC / POST_INVASION / BOTH / DISCORDANT). DISCORDANT SNPs (431) included in ALL enrichment but excluded from pattern-stratified runs or assigned to BOTH.
- **Notes:** NC_058189.1 already excluded. If foreground gene count < 100, expand to n_methods >= 1 (full 54,965).

### SNP-to-Gene Mapping --> Gene-to-GO Mapping (Task 4.1 --> 4.2)
- **Producer:** Task 4.1 script
- **Consumer:** Task 4.2 script
- **Format:** TSV
- **Location:** `results/enrichment/foreground_genes.tsv`, `results/enrichment/background_genes.tsv`
- **Schema (foreground):** gene_id, chrom, gene_start, gene_end, strand, gene_name, ncbi_gene_id, n_outlier_snps, consensus_pattern, detecting_methods, location_type
- **Schema (background):** gene_id, chrom, gene_start, gene_end, strand, gene_name, ncbi_gene_id, n_total_snps
- **Notes:** Foreground is a strict subset of background. gene_id is the LOC* symbol; ncbi_gene_id is the numeric NCBI GeneID (e.g., "123320941") required for direct gene2go lookup in Task 4.2.

### Gene-to-GO Mapping --> GO Enrichment (Task 4.2 --> 4.3)
- **Producer:** Task 4.2 script
- **Consumer:** Task 4.3 enrichment script (clusterProfiler)
- **Format:** TSV
- **Location:** `results/enrichment/gene2go_foreground.tsv`, `results/enrichment/gene2go_background.tsv`
- **Schema:** gene_id, go_id, go_term, go_ontology (BP/MF/CC), evidence_source (direct/orthology_Haxy/orthology_Tcas), ortholog_id, blast_identity
- **Notes:** clusterProfiler enricher() requires TERM2GENE (go_id, gene_id) and TERM2NAME (go_id, go_term) data frames, derived from these tables. Background for ORA = subset of background genes with at least one GO term.

### Gene-GO Cache (Task 4.2 internal, persistent across runs)
- **Producer/Consumer:** Task 4.2 script (reads on start, writes on completion)
- **Format:** TSV + TXT
- **Location:** `results/enrichment/gene_go_cache.tsv`, `results/enrichment/gene_go_cache_meta.txt`
- **Schema (cache):** gene_id, go_id, go_term, go_ontology, evidence_source, ortholog_id, blast_identity, cache_date
- **Schema (meta):** key-value pairs recording MD5 hashes of input files (gene2go, tribolium_gene2go.tsv, BLAST outputs) and the date the cache was last built/updated
- **Invalidation rules:** Cache is invalidated (full rebuild) if any input file hash in meta doesn't match the current file on disk. Otherwise, only genes absent from the cache are annotated and appended. A `--force-rebuild` flag bypasses the cache entirely.
- **Notes:** The cache is keyed on gene_id alone. A gene's GO mapping does not depend on whether it appears in the foreground or background, so the cache is shared across all runs regardless of upstream parameter changes (flanking window, foreground filter). The gene2go_foreground.tsv and gene2go_background.tsv output files are always regenerated from the cache by filtering to the current foreground/background gene lists.

### Population Files (Shared Reference)
- **Location:** `metadata/pop_CHI.txt`, `pop_EEU.txt`, `pop_WEU.txt`, `pop_USA.txt`
- **Format:** One sample ID per line
- **Consumers:** vcftools (FST, diversity), PLINK (sample filtering), R scripts (population labels)
- **Schema:** Sample IDs must match VCF column headers exactly

## 5.2 Expected Value Ranges (QC Reference)

| Metric | Expected | Red Flag |
|---|---|---|
| Sample count | 88 total (CHI=5, EEU=18, WEU=25, USA=40) | Any other number |
| SNP count | ~800,000 | <700K or >900K |
| Chromosomes | 10 (NC_058189.1 through NC_058198.1) | Missing or extra |
| CHI vs invasive FST (weighted) | 0.14-0.15 | <0.10 or >0.25 |
| Among-invasive FST (weighted) | 0.006-0.013 | >0.05 |
| CHI Tajima's D | ~+0.17 | Strongly negative |
| Invasive Tajima's D | -0.15 to -0.33 | Positive |
| Missing data rate (mean) | ~12.6% | >20% |
| Effective CHI n | ~3.3 | <3 |
| High-confidence outlier SNPs (n_methods >= 2) | 3,205 | <100 (foreground too small) |
| Foreground genes (from 3,205 SNPs) | ~200-800 (estimate) | <100 (expand to full set) or >2,000 (flanking too wide) |
| Background genes (testable universe) | ~5,000-15,000 (estimate) | <2,000 (sparse annotation) |
| GO annotation coverage (foreground) | >60% | <40% (consider InterProScan) |
| Enriched GO terms (q<0.05, combined) | 10-100 | 0 (underpowered) or >500 (background bias) |

---

# 6. Current Session

This section holds the in-progress session log entry. It is written during the session and flushed to `ladybug_session_log.Rmd` at session end (see Section 1, "AT SESSION END" for the flush procedure).

[SESSION START]

**Date:** 2026-04-14
**Focus:** Implement Task 4.1 (SNP-to-gene mapping)

### Work Done
1. Read handoff Section 3.1 Task 4.1 spec and concordance summary to confirm foreground set (n_methods >= 2 = 3,205 SNPs)
2. Inspected inputs: master_candidate_table.tsv (54,965 rows, correct schema), ladybug_snps.bim (803,821 SNPs), genomic.gff (14,583 autosomal gene features)
3. Verified BEDTools NOT installed on system. Decision: implement in R using data.table non-equi join rather than install BEDTools. Rationale: fits existing R-centric pipeline; data sizes (~800K SNPs x ~15K genes) are well within data.table's capabilities; avoids external dependency. Note: Section 5.1 data contract mentioned "BEDTools must be installed or installable" -- this has been satisfied by equivalent R implementation.
4. Wrote `scripts/snp_to_gene_mapping.R` (standard header, asymmetric strand-aware flanking 5kb/2kb, intergenic classification, consensus-pattern majority rule with BOTH tiebreak, NCBI Gene ID extraction for downstream gene2go lookup)
5. Ran script successfully. Runtime ~7s on full dataset.
6. Built comprehensive validation suite (30 tests): structural checks (duplicates, NAs, columns, strand, sex-chrom exclusion), cross-reference with master table, strand-aware flanking spot-checks (10 genes), consensus pattern majority rule (20 random genes), background n_total_snps manual BIM verification (5 genes), chromosome distribution, multi-gene SNP distribution, NCBI Gene ID format. All passed except a "FAIL" that turned out to be biology: chromosome NC_058198.1 has 0 high-confidence outliers (only 9 single-method outliers, 279 SNPs total on that chromosome).

### Key Results
- Foreground genes: 2,648 (above 100 red-flag threshold, above 200-800 estimate but consistent with input SNP count)
- Background genes: 11,105 (within 5,000-15,000 expected range)
- Foreground SNP mapping rate: 93.7% (far above the 20% red-flag inversion)
- Intergenic rate: 6.3% (well below 80% red-flag)
- DISCORDANT pattern: 365 genes (need handling in Task 4.3)
- FG/BG ratio CV = 0.11 across chromosomes -> no chromosome-level enrichment bias

### Decisions Made
- Implement in R instead of BEDTools. Logged rationale in script header and handoff.
- Each foreground SNP mapped to all genes whose flanked interval contains it (up to 6 genes for some SNPs due to overlapping gene models). Gene table deduplicates by gene_id, keeping n_outlier_snps as a metadata count.
- ncbi_gene_id column added to foreground and background output (not in original spec) because Task 4.2 gene2go lookup requires the numeric NCBI GeneID, not the LOC* symbol. This is a schema extension; data contract in Section 5.1 should be updated.

### Open Items / For Next Session
- Task 4.2: gene-to-GO mapping. Will use the ncbi_gene_id column for direct gene2go lookup. Verify that existing BLAST output files from December 2025 run are still present and parseable for the orthology-transfer route.

### Files Created
- `scripts/snp_to_gene_mapping.R`
- `results/enrichment/foreground_genes.tsv` (2,648 rows)
- `results/enrichment/background_genes.tsv` (11,105 rows)
- `results/enrichment/snp_to_gene_mapping.tsv` (5,513 rows including intergenic)
- `results/enrichment/snp_gene_summary.txt`

---

**Date:** 2026-04-14 (parallel session)
**Focus:** Implement Task 4.3 (GO ORA, pattern-stratified). Task 4.2 is being worked on simultaneously in another session; Task 4.3 script is written and validated against missing-input guards, but cannot be executed end-to-end until Task 4.2 outputs exist.

### Work Done
1. Read Task 4.3 spec (Section 3.1) and data contracts (Section 5.1 Task 4.2 -> 4.3). Confirmed expected inputs: `gene2go_foreground.tsv`, `gene2go_background.tsv` with schema gene_id, go_id, go_term, go_ontology (BP/MF/CC), evidence_source, ortholog_id, blast_identity.
2. Verified required R packages available locally: clusterProfiler, enrichplot, GO.db (all present). No BiocManager install needed. `optparse` NOT available -> implemented a small base-R key=value CLI parser instead of adding a dependency.
3. Wrote `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` implementing:
   - `enricher()` with TERM2GENE/TERM2NAME built per ontology from `gene2go_background.tsv` (background is superset of foreground; fg_go is used only to compute annotation coverage per pattern).
   - Universe = background genes with >=1 GO term (testable_bg), satisfying the acceptance criterion that ORA background exclude genes with no GO terms.
   - Four foreground sets: ALL, INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH. Iterates over BP/MF/CC.
   - BH FDR (`pAdjustMethod = "BH"`); retains all terms in output, then classifies q <= 0.05 as significant and q <= 0.10 as suggestive.
   - Redundancy reduction: custom `simplify_by_ancestor()` using GO.db's `GOBPANCESTOR`/`GOMFANCESTOR`/`GOCCANCESTOR` (drops any significant term that is an ancestor of another significant term in the same result). Chose this over clusterProfiler::simplify() because the latter needs an OrgDb (none exists for C. septempunctata or T. castaneum on Bioc). This is an OrgDb-free substitute and is documented in the script header.
   - Outputs: `enrichment_results_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`, `enrichment_results_simplified_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`, `enrichment_summary.txt`, `enrichment_log.txt`, and plots in `results/enrichment/plots/`: per-ontology dotplots, per-pattern combined barplots (top 10 per ontology), and the cross-pattern comparison dotplot (BP only).
   - Broad-term flagging: if any of ("metabolic process","binding","cellular process",...) appears in the top 10 of a result, it is flagged in the log and recorded in the summary's broad_hits_top10 column.
4. Dry-run confirmed the script fails gracefully with a BLOCKER log and exit when `gene2go_foreground.tsv` / `gene2go_background.tsv` are absent (current state, pending Task 4.2). Full-file parse passes.

### Decisions Made
- **DISCORDANT handling:** Resolved the deferred question. DISCORDANT genes (n=365) are retained only in the ALL foreground (since ALL = entire high-confidence foreground) and excluded from the three pattern-stratified runs (INVASION_SPECIFIC, POST_INVASION, BOTH). Rationale: DISCORDANT indicates methodological disagreement across FST/OutFLANK/pcadapt, so the pattern has no coherent biological interpretation; reassigning to BOTH would pollute BOTH with methodologically ambiguous genes. This is encoded in `foreground_sets` and logged in `enrichment_log.txt` at run time. Log excerpt: "DISCORDANT genes in foreground: N (retained in ALL; excluded from pattern-stratified runs)". **Flag for Resolved Questions section and for Master Doc decision register.**
- **simplify() substitute:** Ancestor-based pruning (GO.db) chosen over clusterProfiler::simplify() to avoid OrgDb dependency. Trade-off: loses semantic-similarity-based merging; but keeps the most specific term in each lineage, which is the same spirit. If a future three-tier BLAST adds Drosophila orthology, `org.Dm.eg.db`-based simplify() could be reintroduced.
- **CLI:** base-R key=value parser rather than adding `optparse`. Supports `--qcut`, `--top`, `--min-genes`, `--min-gs-size`, `--max-gs-size`.

### Task 4.2 Status Check (2026-04-14, after user note)
- User signaled Task 4.2 "appears done"; verified state: `results/enrichment/gene2go_foreground.tsv` and `results/enrichment/gene2go_background.tsv` DO NOT EXIST. Only `results/enrichment/logs/gene_to_go_mapping.log` is present.
- Log shows: Route A (direct NCBI gene2go, taxid 41139 stream over 112.9M lines) completed, annotating 7,644 of 11,105 background genes. Route B (BLAST orthology) aborted at `makeblastdb` with path-handling error on the iCloud path that contains spaces (`/Users/reina/Library/Mobile Documents/...`). See Section 4.1 Blocker entry.
- Net: Task 4.2 is NOT finished. The two TSVs Task 4.3 consumes were never written, so `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` still cannot execute end-to-end.

### Open Items / For Next Session
- **Task 4.2 is still incomplete.** Fix `scripts/gene_to_go_mapping.py` so `makeblastdb` runs under a path with spaces (stage BLAST DBs in `/tmp/blast_db_biol624/` per the Dec 2025 pattern) OR accept Route-A-only coverage and have the script still emit `gene2go_foreground.tsv` / `gene2go_background.tsv` from Route A alone. Either path unblocks Task 4.3.
- Once those TSVs exist, run `Rscript scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R`.
- Inspect `enrichment_summary.txt` for broad-term dominance (potential background bias signal). If top results are dominated by "metabolic process" / "binding", consider using GO slim or tightening maxGSSize.
- Annotation coverage per pattern is logged at run time; if BOTH (n=177 gene-level) has < 20 annotated foreground genes it will be flagged as underpowered and reported as a null/suggestive result per spec.
- `results/enrichment/gene_go_cache*` (Task 4.2 internal) is not consumed by this script; Task 4.2 side owns it.

### Files Created
- `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R`
- (run-time outputs not yet produced; pending Task 4.2 completion)

### Task 4.3 Executed (2026-04-14, after Task 4.2 completion)
Task 4.2 `gene2go_foreground.tsv` (544 KB, 8,474 rows, 2,170 annotated fg genes) and `gene2go_background.tsv` (1.9 MB, 29,041 rows, 7,915 annotated bg genes) arrived from the anthill run. Schema matched the contract exactly.

**Bug fix applied:** Initial run failed with `Error: object 'ID' not found`. Root cause: `clusterProfiler` masks `dplyr::rename()` with its own version that has different semantics. Fixed by switching to explicit `dplyr::rename()` with quoted column names. Script now runs clean in ~7 seconds.

**Acceptance criteria check:**
- Coverage: ALL 81.9%, INVASION_SPECIFIC 82.6%, POST_INVASION 80.9%, BOTH 88.1% (all above the 20-gene underpowered threshold; none flagged).
- Background (testable, >=1 GO term): 7,915 of 11,105 (71.3%) -- used as ORA universe.
- DISCORDANT (365 genes): excluded from stratified runs; retained in ALL.
- No broad-term contamination in top 10 of any result.

**Significant terms (q <= 0.05):**
- ALL x BP: GO:0006633 fatty acid biosynthetic process (q=0.005, 9/1633); GO:0006468 protein phosphorylation (q=0.039, 63/1633)
- ALL x MF: GO:0050839 cell adhesion molecule binding (q=0.012, 12/1828)
- BOTH x CC: GO:0005912 adherens junction (q=0.026, 4/118)
- INVASION_SPECIFIC: no terms sig at any ontology; none suggestive either.
- POST_INVASION: no terms sig; 2 suggestive BP at q~=0.086 (GO:0030036 actin cytoskeleton organization, GO:0006468 protein phosphorylation).

**Files produced (all in results/enrichment/):**
- 12 x `enrichment_results_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`
- 12 x `enrichment_results_simplified_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`
- `enrichment_summary.txt`, `enrichment_log.txt`
- Plots (17 total): per-pattern/ontology dotplots (12), per-pattern top-term barplots (4), `comparison_dotplot.png` (BP across patterns, 2 terms populated).

**Interpretation notes (flag for Master Doc, not final):**
- Signal in ALL is driven primarily by the larger POST_INVASION subset (1,410 of 2,648 fg genes), plus contributions from BOTH/INVASION_SPECIFIC. That the stratified POST_INVASION run itself is null-to-suggestive suggests the ALL signal depends on combined power rather than one pattern's coherent enrichment.
- Adherens junction enrichment in BOTH (4/118 genes, q=0.026) is worth spot-checking -- small counts, but biologically interpretable given invasion/dispersal + cell adhesion literature.
- No enrichment in INVASION_SPECIFIC despite 575 annotated genes suggests invasion-period selection is polygenic/diffuse at the GO level, not pathway-concentrated.

### Files Created (this session, final)
- `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` (committed 2026-04-14 as cfe4fc8; bug-fix rename change to be committed now)
- `results/enrichment/enrichment_results_*.tsv` (24 files)
- `results/enrichment/enrichment_summary.txt`, `enrichment_log.txt`
- `results/enrichment/plots/*.png` (17 plots)

### Task 4.3 Results -- Detailed Summary for Claude.ai Discussion (2026-04-14)

This block is written self-contained so a fresh conversation (e.g. Claude.ai) can interpret the results without re-deriving project context. Use alongside Master Doc for methodology detail.

#### Study context (one-paragraph recap)
Population genomics of the invasive ladybird beetle *Coccinella septempunctata* (88 samples, 4 continent groups: CHI native, EEU/WEU/USA invasive). Phase 3 produced 54,965 cross-method outlier SNPs via FST + OutFLANK + pcadapt, of which 3,205 are high-confidence (n_methods >= 2). Those SNPs were labeled by which comparison flagged them (CHI-vs-invasive only -> INVASION_SPECIFIC; among-invasive only -> POST_INVASION; both -> BOTH; methods disagreed -> DISCORDANT). Phase 4 (Tasks 4.1-4.3) mapped SNPs to genes, annotated genes with GO terms, and ran ORA stratified by pattern. Task 4.3 is the ORA step.

#### Method (Task 4.3)
- **Tool:** clusterProfiler::enricher() with custom TERM2GENE / TERM2NAME per ontology.
- **Foregrounds:** ALL (2,648 genes / 2,170 annotated), INVASION_SPECIFIC (696 / 575), POST_INVASION (1,410 / 1,141), BOTH (177 / 156). DISCORDANT (365 genes) retained in ALL only, excluded from stratified runs because the pattern itself reflects methodological disagreement rather than biology.
- **Background / universe:** 7,915 autosomal genes with >=1 GO term (71.3% of 11,105 testable gene universe). Genes without any GO term are untestable by ORA and excluded from the universe.
- **Ontologies:** BP, MF, CC tested separately (3 tests per foreground = 12 tests total).
- **FDR:** Benjamini-Hochberg. Significance q <= 0.05; suggestive q <= 0.10 (per spec).
- **Gene-set-size filter:** minGSSize=5, maxGSSize=500 (defaults).
- **Simplification:** OrgDb-free ancestor-pruning via GO.db (keeps most specific term in each lineage). Note: user subsequently modified the script to add an rrvgo-based redundancy-reduction pathway using org.Dm.eg.db as a phylogenetic IC proxy (see script header); that variant has not been re-run yet.

#### Results headline
Four significant GO terms across 12 tests (q <= 0.05). All flagged terms are biologically specific (no "metabolic process" / "binding" broad-term contamination).

| Pattern | Ontology | GO ID | Term | fg/bg ratio | q-value |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| ALL | BP | GO:0006633 | fatty acid biosynthetic process | 9/1633 vs 9/5715 | 0.005 |
| ALL | BP | GO:0006468 | protein phosphorylation | 63/1633 vs 149/5715 | 0.039 |
| ALL | MF | GO:0050839 | cell adhesion molecule binding | 12/1828 vs 15/6540 | 0.012 |
| BOTH | CC | GO:0005912 | adherens junction | 4/118 vs 18/5633 | 0.026 |

INVASION_SPECIFIC returned no terms significant or suggestive at any ontology. POST_INVASION returned no significant terms; 2 suggestive BP at q~=0.086 (GO:0030036 actin cytoskeleton organization; GO:0006468 protein phosphorylation). BOTH x BP, BOTH x MF both null. ALL x CC null.

#### Suggestive clusters (q <= 0.10, used as supporting evidence)
- **ALL x BP (2 sig + 12 suggestive total):** fatty acid biosynthesis (sig), protein phosphorylation (sig), cGMP biosynthesis, signal transduction, small GTPase signaling, actin cytoskeleton organization, innate immune response, fatty acid beta-oxidation, cell surface receptor signaling, GPCR/adenylate cyclase signaling, NHEJ double-strand break repair, N-glycan processing.
- **ALL x MF (1 sig + 11 suggestive):** cell adhesion molecule binding (sig), guanyl-nucleotide exchange factor activity, guanylate cyclase activity, fatty acid synthase activity (5/5 genes, cross-confirms BP fatty-acid signal), 3-oxoacyl-ACP synthase activity, ATP-dependent chromatin remodeler activity, molecular adaptor activity, hexosyltransferase activity, G-protein beta/gamma-subunit complex binding, chitin binding, small GTPase binding.
- **BOTH x CC (1 sig + 6 suggestive):** adherens junction (sig), plasma membrane, microvillus, filopodium, stress fiber, filamentous actin, Z disc. Coherent cell-junction/cytoskeleton theme.
- **POST_INVASION x BP (0 sig + 2 suggestive):** actin cytoskeleton organization, protein phosphorylation.

#### QC status
- Annotation coverage per pattern 80.9-88.1% of fg genes (above the 20-gene underpowered threshold). BOTH (156 annotated) is smallest but still well over threshold.
- Foreground annotation rate (81.9%) exceeds background rate (71.3%). This is the normal direction of bias (outlier genes tend to be conserved, which have better annotation) and reduces rather than inflates enrichment signal.
- Broad-term flagger found zero hits in top 10 of any of the 12 tests. Top hits are specific biology, not housekeeping categories.
- Foreground is a strict subset of background. Universe is the 7,915 testable background genes (not the full 11,105), per spec.
- No enricher errors, no overflow, no ID-space mismatches. Pipeline produced all 24 results TSVs + 17 plots cleanly.

#### Open interpretive questions for Claude.ai

1. **Is 4 sig terms / 12 tests a red flag, or expected?** Heuristics on this vary. Relevant factors: (a) polygenic adaptation rarely concentrates in pathways; (b) pattern stratification divides power across 3 subsets; (c) ORA is conservative relative to GSEA; (d) non-model Coleoptera GO coverage is shallow and orthology-transferred terms skew conserved. But the sparseness could also reflect real architectural properties (polygenic local adaptation) or a methodological loss of signal. Opinion welcome on whether the current reporting frame ("weak but coherent signal") is honest or overconfident.

2. **Why does ALL show signal but no pattern-stratified subset reproduces it?** Possible readings:
   - Power: splitting 2,648 genes into 696 / 1,410 / 177 leaves each subset underpowered for small-effect terms; ALL's signal is the statistical pooling benefit, not a pattern-specific enrichment.
   - Mixture: ALL is enriched because *different* small subsets contribute *different* terms whose signals compound; no single subset carries a term's full signal.
   - Dilution: ALL contains DISCORDANT (365 genes) which pad foreground size and may dilute real pattern-level signal.
   - Our current interpretation: mostly (a) with some (b). Would welcome alternate readings, especially on whether (c) is biasing anything.

3. **Is the BOTH x CC adherens junction hit biologically meaningful at 4/118 foreground ratio?** Fold enrichment ~10x (4/118 vs 18/5633 -> 3.4% vs 0.32%) is strong; count is small. Adherens junction + the suggestive cytoskeletal cluster (filopodium, stress fiber, microvillus, filamentous actin) forms a coherent module. But with n=4 genes driving the sig result, spot-checking is warranted -- do those 4 genes also appear in other suggestive lists (plasma membrane, cytoskeleton)? Would benefit from Claude.ai perspective on whether cell-junction biology is a known/expected target in invasive-insect selection-scan literature (Harmonia axyridis, Drosophila suzukii, Ceratitis capitata, Aedes aegypti all have relevant comparisons).

4. **INVASION_SPECIFIC null result -- biology or power?** 575 annotated genes with zero sig and zero suggestive terms across all ontologies. Competing readings:
   - Biological: invasion-phase selection is highly polygenic; selection acted on many loci with small per-locus GO coherence (standing-variation sweeps).
   - Methodological: CHI n=4 limits statistical power of the CHI-vs-invasive comparison itself, so INVASION_SPECIFIC SNP labels are noisy at the source; downstream enrichment therefore gets no signal because the SNP labels are partially noise.
   - Annotation: GO annotations transferred via Harmonia-Tribolium orthology are conserved-biology-biased; invasion-specific adaptations may involve species-specific or rapidly-evolving genes that escape annotation.
   - Need Claude.ai to weight these and suggest follow-up (e.g. inspect INVASION_SPECIFIC genes by manual annotation of top-FST outliers; check for Tol/Dmel-undetectable immune or chemosensory gene families).

5. **POST_INVASION's 2 suggestive BP terms (actin cytoskeleton, protein phosphorylation) -- story or noise?** These echo the ALL-level signals exactly. Could reflect (a) real local-adaptation biology around cell motility / kinase-mediated signaling, or (b) genes that were already in the ALL top terms leaking into the POST_INVASION subset because POST_INVASION dominates ALL by raw count. How to disentangle?

6. **Recommended sensitivity analyses before manuscript freeze?** Candidates:
   - q <= 0.10 reporting (doubles the populated story without statistical dishonesty).
   - Loosen minGSSize=3, maxGSSize=1000.
   - Add GSEA-style complement on a per-gene selection score (e.g. max -log10 p across methods) using fgsea. Non-trivial to set up (need per-gene summary stat) but often surfaces polygenic signal ORA misses.
   - GO slim reduction as a second-look at broad-category enrichment.
   - Per-term per-gene detail reporting (manuscript table): the 9 fatty-acid genes, 12 cell-adhesion-binding genes, 4 adherens-junction genes. Feasible at these sizes.
   - rrvgo-based parent-term reduction (script was updated to support this; not yet re-run). Would give a more REVIGO-like visual summary for the manuscript.

7. **Manuscript framing.** Current proposed language: "ORA identified a small number of significantly enriched terms (n=4, q <= 0.05) concentrated in lipid metabolism, cell adhesion, and cytoskeletal/junction biology. A broader suggestive signature (q <= 0.10) reinforces these themes across ontologies. Pattern-stratified analyses reveal that cell-junction enrichment is specific to the BOTH subset (SNPs flagged in both CHI-vs-invasive and among-invasive comparisons), while the lipid-metabolism and adhesion signals are most visible in the pooled analysis." Want Claude.ai feedback on whether this is honest, whether it over- or under-claims, and whether the ordering/emphasis serves the invasion-biology story.

#### Files Claude.ai should reference
- Methods: `scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` (header has full method description and updates)
- Top-line results: `results/enrichment/enrichment_summary.txt`
- Full results: `results/enrichment/enrichment_results_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv` (12 files)
- Simplified results (ancestor-pruned parents): `results/enrichment/enrichment_results_simplified_{pattern}_{ontology}.tsv`
- Log with parameters/diagnostics: `results/enrichment/enrichment_log.txt`
- Upstream inputs: `results/enrichment/gene2go_{foreground,background}.tsv`, `foreground_genes.tsv`, `background_genes.tsv`
- Annotation coverage provenance: `results/enrichment/go_mapping_summary.txt`
- Visuals: `results/enrichment/plots/` (dotplot_{pattern}_{ontology}.png, barplot_top_terms_{pattern}.png, comparison_dotplot.png)

[SESSION END]

### Task 4.2 Successful Anthill Run (2026-04-14, later same day)
First successful end-to-end Task 4.2 run after the earlier makeblastdb-spaces failure. Work moved from local mesx/iCloud path to anthill cluster scratchF mount (no spaces in path), which resolved the BLAST DB build issue without any code change.

**Cluster discovery:** Anthill runs Slurm (debug partition, node12, node14-17) and PBS/Torque (batch queue, node18+) side by side on the same `/usr/scratchF` mount. Slurm `debug` was heavily contended by another user (~14 running, 7 queued); PBS `batch` was uncontested. First submission via Slurm (jobs 68850, 68852) never dispatched; PBS submission dispatched instantly.

**Submission:**
- Script: `scripts/submit_task4_2.pbs` (paths fixed this session from stale `/sdd/home2/...` to `/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025`; `/home2/` conda path fixed to `${HOME}/miniconda3`)
- Command: `qsub scripts/submit_task4_2.pbs`
- Job ID: `7446.anthill`
- Node: node20
- Resources: ppn=16, mem=16gb, walltime=03:00:00 (actual wall 00:03:26; far faster than estimated)
- Email policy: `#PBS -m abe` (abort/begin/end) + submit-time sendmail + 30-min in-job monitor to rhastings5624@sdsu.edu

**Results (from `results/enrichment/go_mapping_summary.txt`):**
- Foreground coverage: **81.9%** (2170/2648) -- exceeds 60% target and exceeds the 68% mesx Route-A-only baseline
  - Route A only: 2,095; Route B only: 75; both: 0 (by construction, Route B only attempted on Route-A misses); neither: 478
  - By ontology -- BP: 1,633; MF: 1,828; CC: 1,603
- Background coverage: **71.3%** (7915/11105)
  - Route A only: 7,644 (matches mesx Route A baseline of 68.8% exactly -- good reproducibility signal)
  - Route B only: 271; both: 0; neither: 3,190
- Unique GO terms: foreground union 2,340; background union 4,221
- Cache: 0 hits (first anthill run), 7,915 misses written. `gene_go_cache.tsv` + `gene_go_cache_meta.txt` now present for future-run caching.

**BLAST stage stats:**
- Route B candidates: 3,461 genes; 2,499 with protein sequence, 962 without
- Step 1 (Csep -> Harmonia axyridis @ 50% identity, evalue 1e-10): **1,658** hits in 69s
- Step 2 (Harmonia -> Tribolium castaneum @ 30% identity, evalue 1e-10): **1,208** hits in 53s
- Genes with GO inherited via two-step orthology: 271

**Output files (`results/enrichment/`):**
- `gene2go_foreground.tsv` -- 544,833 bytes, 8,475 lines
- `gene2go_background.tsv` -- 1,874,716 bytes, 29,042 lines
- `gene_go_cache.tsv` -- 2,331,117 bytes, 32,232 lines
- `gene_go_cache_meta.txt` -- 487 bytes
- `go_mapping_summary.txt` -- 1,162 bytes
- `logs/task4_2.out` -- 3,117 bytes
- `logs/task4_2.err` -- 72 bytes (single harmless esmtp config warning; no job failure)

**Observations / caveats:**
- "Both routes = 0" is structural (script only runs Route B on Route-A misses), not a data issue. [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED] if we want an independent full-set Route B run to validate agreement with Route A.
- 478 foreground + 3,190 background genes remain unannotated. Candidates for a Route C (InterProScan domain-based) if downstream enrichment power is low.
- BLAST was dramatically faster than my initial 60-120 min estimate because query sets are ~2.5k proteins and DBs are ~20k each -- small for modern blastp. Next time don't estimate without checking query/db sizes.
- Task 4.3 (`scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R`) is now unblocked; all its required inputs exist.

**Next steps:**
- Review outputs before committing to git (user gated this explicitly).
- rsync selected outputs to local for Task 4.3 development (see "Local development rsync list" below).
- Run `Rscript scripts/go_enrichment_ora.R` once outputs are validated.

### Local development rsync list (Task 4.2 -> Task 4.3 handoff)
For continuing Task 4.3 development locally, pull the following from anthill. Everything lives under `/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/`. Destination assumed to be the local project root.

Required (Task 4.3 consumes these directly):
- `results/enrichment/gene2go_foreground.tsv`
- `results/enrichment/gene2go_background.tsv`
- `results/enrichment/go_mapping_summary.txt`

Strongly recommended (diagnostics + provenance):
- `results/enrichment/logs/task4_2.out` (has the 7-stage log, BLAST counts, timestamps)
- `results/enrichment/logs/task4_2.err` (1-line harmless warning; keep for audit trail)

Optional (large; only pull if re-running locally or auditing cache logic):
- `results/enrichment/gene_go_cache.tsv` (~2.3 MB)
- `results/enrichment/gene_go_cache_meta.txt` (487 B; tiny, worth pulling if you pull the cache file)

Do NOT rsync:
- `data/go_annotations/gene2go` (9.96 GB NCBI file; only needed for re-running Route A)
- `data/blast_db/` (rebuild from protein.faa if needed)
- Existing local copies of `results/enrichment/{foreground,background}_genes.tsv` -- those are the Task 4.1 outputs and are already on local.

One-liner (run from local project root, assumes `anthill:` ssh alias resolves to the right host):
```
rsync -avz --progress \
    'anthill:/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/results/enrichment/gene2go_foreground.tsv' \
    'anthill:/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/results/enrichment/gene2go_background.tsv' \
    'anthill:/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/results/enrichment/go_mapping_summary.txt' \
    'anthill:/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/results/enrichment/logs/task4_2.out' \
    'anthill:/usr/scratchF/rhastings/BIOL624_Project_Fall_2025/results/enrichment/logs/task4_2.err' \
    results/enrichment/
```
(Move `task4_2.*` into `results/enrichment/logs/` locally if the `logs/` subdir exists; the flat rsync above drops them into `results/enrichment/`.) [FOLLOW-UP NEEDED] confirm your ssh alias for anthill and adjust accordingly.

[SESSION END 2026-04-14 PM]
